english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Romans

verses.latin_vulgate: Obsecro itaque vos fratres per misericordiam Dei, ut exhibeatis corpora vestra hostiam viventem, sanctam, Deo placentem, rationabile obsequium vestrum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παρακαλῶ οὖν ὑμᾶς ἀδελφοί διὰ τῶν οἰκτιρμῶν τοῦ θεοῦ παραστῆσαι τὰ σώματα ὑμῶν θυσίαν ζῶσαν ἁγίαν εὐάρεστον τῷ θεῷ τὴν λογικὴν λατρείαν ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: I beseech you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that ye present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable unto God, *which is* your reasonable service.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nolite conformari huic sæculo, sed reformamini in novitate sensus vestri: ut probetis quæ sit voluntas Dei bona, et beneplacens, et perfecta.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μὴ συσχηματίζεσθε τῷ αἰῶνι τούτῳ ἀλλὰ μεταμορφοῦσθε τῇ ἀνακαινώσει τοῦ νοός ὑμῶν, εἰς τὸ δοκιμάζειν ὑμᾶς τί τὸ θέλημα τοῦ θεοῦ τὸ ἀγαθὸν καὶ εὐάρεστον καὶ τέλειον
verses.king_james: And be not conformed to this world: but be ye transformed by the renewing of your mind, that ye may prove what *is* that good, and acceptable, and perfect, will of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dico enim per gratiam quæ data est mihi, omnibus qui sunt inter vos, non plus sapere quam oportet sapere, sed sapere ad sobrietatem: et unicuique sicut Deus divisit mensuram fidei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγω γὰρ διὰ τῆς χάριτος τῆς δοθείσης μοι παντὶ τῷ ὄντι ἐν ὑμῖν μὴ ὑπερφρονεῖν παρ᾽ ὃ δεῖ φρονεῖν ἀλλὰ φρονεῖν εἰς τὸ σωφρονεῖν ἑκάστῳ ὡς ὁ θεὸς ἐμέρισεν μέτρον πίστεως
verses.king_james: For I say, through the grace given unto me, to every man that is among you, not to think *of himself* more highly than he ought to think; but to think soberly, according as God hath dealt to every man the measure of faith.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut enim in uno corpore multa membra habemus, omnia autem membra non eumdem actum habent:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθάπερ γὰρ ἐν ἑνὶ σώματι μέλη πολλὰ ἔχομεν τὰ δὲ μέλη πάντα οὐ τὴν αὐτὴν ἔχει πρᾶξιν
verses.king_james: For as we have many members in one body, and all members have not the same office:

verses.latin_vulgate: ita multi unum corpus sumus in Christo, singuli autem alter alterius membra.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως οἱ πολλοὶ ἓν σῶμά ἐσμεν ἐν χριστῷ ὁ δὲ καθ᾽ εἷς ἀλλήλων μέλη
verses.king_james: So we, *being* many, are one body in Christ, and every one members one of another.

verses.latin_vulgate: Habentes autem donationes secundum gratiam, quæ data est nobis, differentes: sive prophetiam secundum rationem fidei,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔχοντες δὲ χαρίσματα κατὰ τὴν χάριν τὴν δοθεῖσαν ἡμῖν διάφορα εἴτε προφητείαν κατὰ τὴν ἀναλογίαν τῆς πίστεως
verses.king_james: Having then gifts differing according to the grace that is given to us, whether prophecy, *let us prophesy* according to the proportion of faith;

verses.latin_vulgate: sive ministerium in ministrando, sive qui docet in doctrina,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴτε διακονίαν ἐν τῇ διακονίᾳ εἴτε ὁ διδάσκων ἐν τῇ διδασκαλίᾳ
verses.king_james: Or ministry, *let us wait* on *our* ministering: or he that teacheth, on teaching;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui exhortatur in exhortando, qui tribuit in simplicitate, qui præest in sollicitudine, qui miseretur in hilaritate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴτε ὁ παρακαλῶν ἐν τῇ παρακλήσει ὁ μεταδιδοὺς ἐν ἁπλότητι ὁ προϊστάμενος ἐν σπουδῇ ὁ ἐλεῶν ἐν ἱλαρότητι
verses.king_james: Or he that exhorteth, on exhortation: he that giveth, *let him do it* with simplicity; he that ruleth, with diligence; he that sheweth mercy, with cheerfulness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dilectio sine simulatione: odientes malum, adhærentes bono:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ ἀγάπη ἀνυπόκριτος ἀποστυγοῦντες τὸ πονηρόν κολλώμενοι τῷ ἀγαθῷ
verses.king_james: *Let* love be without dissimulation. Abhor that which is evil; cleave to that which is good.

verses.latin_vulgate: caritate fraternitatis invicem diligentes: honore invicem prævenientes:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῇ φιλαδελφίᾳ εἰς ἀλλήλους φιλόστοργοι τῇ τιμῇ ἀλλήλους προηγούμενοι
verses.king_james: *Be* kindly affectioned one to another with brotherly love; in honour preferring one another;

verses.latin_vulgate: sollicitudine non pigri: spiritu ferventes: Domino servientes:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῇ σπουδῇ μὴ ὀκνηροί τῷ πνεύματι ζέοντες τῷ καιρῷ δουλεύοντες
verses.king_james: Not slothful in business; fervent in spirit; serving the Lord;

verses.latin_vulgate: spe gaudentes: in tribulatione patientes: orationi instantes:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῇ ἐλπίδι χαίροντες τῇ θλίψει ὑπομένοντες τῇ προσευχῇ προσκαρτεροῦντες
verses.king_james: Rejoicing in hope; patient in tribulation; continuing instant in prayer;

verses.latin_vulgate: necessitatibus sanctorum communicantes: hospitalitatem sectantes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῖς χρείαις τῶν ἁγίων κοινωνοῦντες τὴν φιλοξενίαν διώκοντες
verses.king_james: Distributing to the necessity of saints; given to hospitality.

verses.latin_vulgate: Benedicite persequentibus vos: benedicite, et nolite maledicere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὐλογεῖτε τοὺς διώκοντας ὑμᾶς εὐλογεῖτε καὶ μὴ καταρᾶσθε
verses.king_james: Bless them which persecute you: bless, and curse not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gaudere cum gaudentibus, flere cum flentibus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χαίρειν μετὰ χαιρόντων καὶ κλαίειν μετὰ κλαιόντων
verses.king_james: Rejoice with them that do rejoice, and weep with them that weep.

verses.latin_vulgate: idipsum invicem sentientes: non alta sapientes, sed humilibus consentientes. Nolite esse prudentes apud vosmetipsos:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ αὐτὸ εἰς ἀλλήλους φρονοῦντες μὴ τὰ ὑψηλὰ φρονοῦντες ἀλλὰ τοῖς ταπεινοῖς συναπαγόμενοι μὴ γίνεσθε φρόνιμοι παρ᾽ ἑαυτοῖς
verses.king_james: *Be* of the same mind one toward another. Mind not high things, but condescend to men of low estate. Be not wise in your own conceits.

verses.latin_vulgate: nulli malum pro malo reddentes: providentes bona non tantum coram Deo, sed etiam coram omnibus hominibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μηδενὶ κακὸν ἀντὶ κακοῦ ἀποδιδόντες προνοούμενοι καλὰ ἐνώπιον πάντων ἀνθρώπων
verses.king_james: Recompense to no man evil for evil. Provide things honest in the sight of all men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si fieri potest, quod ex vobis est, cum omnibus hominibus pacem habentes:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δυνατόν τὸ ἐξ ὑμῶν μετὰ πάντων ἀνθρώπων εἰρηνεύοντες
verses.king_james: If it be possible, as much as lieth in you, live peaceably with all men.

verses.latin_vulgate: non vosmetipsos defendentes carissimi, sed date locum iræ. Scriptum est enim: Mihi vindicta: ego retribuam, dicit Dominus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ ἑαυτοὺς ἐκδικοῦντες ἀγαπητοί ἀλλὰ δότε τόπον τῇ ὀργῇ γέγραπται γάρ ἐμοὶ ἐκδίκησις ἐγὼ ἀνταποδώσω λέγει κύριος
verses.king_james: Dearly beloved, avenge not yourselves, but *rather* give place unto wrath: for it is written, Vengeance *is* mine; I will repay, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed si esurierit inimicus tuus, ciba illum: si sitit, potum da illi: hoc enim faciens, carbones ignis congeres super caput ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν οὖν πεινᾷ ὁ ἐχθρός σου ψώμιζε αὐτόν ἐὰν διψᾷ πότιζε αὐτόν τοῦτο γὰρ ποιῶν ἄνθρακας πυρὸς σωρεύσεις ἐπὶ τὴν κεφαλὴν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Therefore if thine enemy hunger, feed him; if he thirst, give him drink: for in so doing thou shalt heap coals of fire on his head.

verses.latin_vulgate: Noli vinci a malo, sed vince in bono malum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ νικῶ ὑπὸ τοῦ κακοῦ ἀλλὰ νίκα ἐν τῷ ἀγαθῷ τὸ κακόν
verses.king_james: Be not overcome of evil, but overcome evil with good.
